items.prompt: What is the core idea of the active-learning loop?
choices: Label every unlabeled item before any training begins
Iteratively query informative unlabeled items, label them, and retrain
Replace all human labels with random synthetic labels
Train once on a fixed set and never request new labels

items.prompt: Example 11.1 queues a cat–dog image when predicted probabilities are near a 0.51/0.49 tie.
items.explain: Uncertainty sampling targets near-ties that reshape the decision boundary.

items.prompt: Margin sampling primarily ranks instances by which criterion?
choices: The smallest gap between the top two predicted class probabilities
The longest filename in the unlabeled pool
The oldest timestamp in the collection log
items.explain: Example 11.2 and the margin-sampling definition use the top-two probability gap.

items.prompt: In Example 11.3, why is entropy high when class probabilities are 0.4, 0.3, and 0.3?
items.rubric: No class dominates / flat distribution / high predictive uncertainty.
items.sample_answer: No class dominates; the flat distribution yields high predictive entropy.

items.prompt: Example 11.4 uses active learning so radiologists label every MRI scan in the pool.
items.explain: The example queries ambiguous or rare-tumor scans, cutting expert hours on easy cases.
